behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is parser/resolver/query-builder handoff across modules, not one pure function. Rejecting bogus shortcut: not choosing Integration just because feature is multi-module; specifically, missing transitive deps appears when extraction output is handed to vulnerability-check input. Integration-brake: no required real network/OS/subprocess in behavior text, no AI output dependency, no multi-agent chain. Rejection-gate: System was considered, but behavior text says only extraction/inclusion, not real API/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner components together against fixture manifests (with nested/transitive deps) and a stub vuln client; assert every expected direct+transitive package/version is present in outbound check set.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: Failure can depend on real vulnerability-API query semantics (version-range parameters/encoding/interpretation) over real HTTP. A mock can always echo expected range matches and hide protocol/contract mismatches. Rejecting bogus shortcut: not lowering to Integration merely because a stub endpoint exists; that would miss real API behavior differences.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real vuln DB sandbox/staging endpoint, submit manifests with same package name but different constrained ranges; assert returned CVEs differ appropriately by exact version range.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic ordering logic within a local function/object boundary; no module handoff, infra, or model dependence is required to expose failure. Rejecting bogus shortcut: not escalating just because scanner is a larger integration feature.
behaviors.test_description: Feed unsorted CVE objects into sort function; assert strict severity rank order and descending CVSS tiebreak within each severity bucket.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is in parse-edit-serialize wiring (and often formatting/structure preservation), not just a single pure comparator. Rejecting bogus shortcut: not choosing lowest because mocks exist; unit tests of patch function alone can miss serializer side effects. Integration-brake: behavior does not require real network/subprocess, no AI output, no multi-agent chain. Rejection-gate: System considered, but behavior text is about content correctness of manifest modification, not OS-specific file-event behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use real manifest fixtures, run full update pipeline for one vulnerable dep, and diff before/after to assert exactly one version field changed and all other entries are byte-for-byte or structurally unchanged per format rules.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires creation on a real repository, crossing git + remote API boundaries. Mocks can hide auth/branch/protection/diff-upload mismatches. Rejecting bogus shortcut: cannot demote to Integration when requirement text says "real repository."
behaviors.test_description: In a real test repo, run scanner on a known vulnerable dep and verify an actual PR is opened with exactly one dependency version change in the diff.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Semver major-bump detection is deterministic local logic and fails within one function boundary. Rejecting bogus shortcut: not escalating due to nearby PR/network behavior in the broader feature.
behaviors.test_description: Table-drive semver pairs (including pre-release/build metadata edge cases) and assert breaking-change=true only when major version increases per semver rules.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at orchestration boundary: semver decision must gate PR module and propagate status to output/report module. Rejecting bogus shortcut: not choosing Integration by default for multi-module features; specifically, this behavior is the handoff/gating contract itself. Integration-brake: no required real OS/network in behavior text (PR client can be asserted not-called), no AI output dependency, no multi-agent chain. Rejection-gate: System considered, but text requires "skipped" and "flag surfaced," which can be verified without real PR API call.
behaviors.test_description: Wire decision engine + PR service interface + report renderer with a breaking-change input; assert PR creation method is not invoked and scanner output contains review-required flag.
